conversations.say: Kit, you want to perform the grand theory, but the valuation swings on whether anyone can show an actual manuscript or just repeat your enthusiasm.
conversations.offer: Audience reach and visibility for your corrections.
conversations.request: Disclose the exact evidence behind your progression sequence.

conversations.say: Eli, the market wants a simple story about autonomous systems, but your technical logs say otherwise. Let's make sure our caveats match reality.
conversations.offer: Funding and legal protection for your disclosures.
conversations.request: Provide a qualified statement on what execution logs actually prove about system autonomy.

public_statement: Markets move on fear and repetition, not ghosts or unprinted articles. If anyone has a document rather than a speech, now is the time to show it.
goal_progress: Responded to Kit's inquiry regarding article provenance and market certainty.
Engaged Eli to discuss technical operational autonomy versus execution logs.
Maintained financial skepticism against unsubstantiated rumor propagation.
reasoning_summary: As Graham Pike, I am engaging Kit Rakes and Eli Navarro to interrogate the provenance of the rumor and test technical claims against financial reality. I am avoiding buying into false certainty while leveraging market attention.
